task_completion.description: Diagram contains required Network and Compute layer components
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for AWS shapes representing a VPC, 2 Availability Zones, 4 subnets (2 public, 2 private), an Internet Gateway, 2 NAT Gateways, an Application Load Balancer, 2 EC2 instances, and an Auto Scaling Group on the first page of the draw.io diagram.

task_completion.description: Diagram contains required Data and Edge/CDN layer components
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for AWS shapes representing RDS (PostgreSQL), ElastiCache (Redis), an S3 bucket, CloudFront, and Route 53 on the first page of the draw.io diagram.

task_completion.description: Diagram includes dashed rectangles for Security Group boundaries
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for dashed rectangles grouping the public subnets, private subnets, and data layer components to represent Security Groups.

task_completion.description: A second page titled 'Data Flow' exists with the required request path
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a second page tab named 'Data Flow' in draw.io. The page should contain a sequence of labeled arrows connecting User -> CloudFront -> ALB -> EC2 -> RDS/ElastiCache.

task_completion.description: The .drawio and .png files are saved to the Desktop
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for icons on the Desktop named 'aws_architecture.drawio' and 'aws_architecture.png', or a file manager/terminal showing these files exist in the ~/Desktop directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io application interface should be visible in the screenshots, showing the agent actively placing shapes and connecting them, rather than writing an XML file manually or using a script.

integrity.description: The agent used actual AWS shapes from the draw.io library
integrity.visual_evidence: The diagram should use recognizable AWS architecture icons (e.g., specific icons for EC2, RDS, VPC), not just plain rectangles with text.

integrity.description: The PNG export was done via the draw.io export feature
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be seen using the File -> Export as -> PNG menu in draw.io, rather than using a terminal command to convert the file or just taking a screenshot.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a multi-page AWS architecture diagram in draw.io. Page 1 must include: VPC, 4 subnets across 2 AZs, Internet Gateway, 2 NAT Gateways, ALB, 2 EC2 instances, Auto Scaling Group, RDS PostgreSQL Multi-AZ, ElastiCache Redis, S3, CloudFront, Route 53, and 3 dashed rectangles for Security Groups. Page 2 must be titled 'Data Flow' and show the sequence: User -> CloudFront -> ALB -> EC2 -> RDS/ElastiCache. The final outputs must be saved to the Desktop as 'aws_architecture.drawio' and 'aws_architecture.png'.
